(October 17, 1892 – June 4, 1946) was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") lawyer and mayor of [Cernăuți](/wiki/Chernivtsi "Chernivtsi") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), known for saving 20,000 [Jews](/wiki/Jews "Jews") of [Bukovina](/wiki/Bukovina "Bukovina") from deportation.

**Traian Popovici** (October 17, 1892 – June 4, 1946) was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") lawyer and mayor of [Cernăuți](/wiki/Chernivtsi "Chernivtsi") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), known for saving 20,000 [Jews](/wiki/Jews "Jews") of [Bukovina](/wiki/Bukovina "Bukovina") from deportation.

## Life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Traian_Popovici&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life")]

Popovici was born in [Rușii Mănăstioarei](/wiki/Ude%C8%99ti "Udești") village of the [Duchy of Bukovina](/wiki/Duchy_of_Bukovina "Duchy of Bukovina") in the [Austro-Hungarian Empire](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Empire "Austro-Hungarian Empire"). He was the son of Andrei Popovich of [Udești](/wiki/Ude%C8%99ti "Udești") and grandson of Ioan Popovich, a famous priest, who, in 1777, refused to take the oath to the [Austrian Empire](/wiki/Austrian_Empire "Austrian Empire").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) His uncle was [Dorimedont Popovici](/w/index.php?title=Dorimedont_Popovici&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dorimedont Popovici (page does not exist)") [[ro](https://ro.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dorimedont_Popovici "ro:Dorimedont Popovici")] (1873–1950), who became a minister for [Bucovina](/wiki/Bucovina "Bucovina") under the government led by General [Alexandru Averescu](/wiki/Alexandru_Averescu "Alexandru Averescu").

He studied at the Suceava high school (1903–1911), then enrolled at the Faculty of Law of the [University of Chernowitz](/wiki/Chernivtsi_University "Chernivtsi University"), which he graduated at the end of [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"). He was a former president of *[Societatea Academică Junimea](/wiki/Societatea_Academic%C4%83_Junimea "Societatea Academică Junimea")*. In 1908, while a high school student, he crossed from Austria-Hungary into [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania") illegally, in order to see [Nicolae Iorga](/wiki/Nicolae_Iorga "Nicolae Iorga") who was visiting the town of [Burdujeni](/wiki/Suceava "Suceava").[[5]](#cite_note-5) When World War I started, he went to Romania and enlisted in the [Romanian Army](/wiki/Romanian_Land_Forces "Romanian Land Forces"), fighting until the end of the war. After World War I, he settled briefly in [Chișinău](/wiki/Chi%C8%99in%C4%83u "Chișinău"), where he was secretary at "Our House" organization that dealt with land reform.

In the [interwar period](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period"), he worked as a lawyer in the city of Cernăuți. He was the head of the [Câmpulung Moldovenesc](/wiki/C%C3%A2mpulung_Moldovenesc "Câmpulung Moldovenesc") branch of the [National Agrarian Party](/wiki/National_Agrarian_Party "National Agrarian Party").[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2) In 1935, he was the founder of the Cernăuți branch of the [far-right](/wiki/Far-right "Far-right") [National Christian Party](/wiki/National_Christian_Party "National Christian Party").[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1) After the [Soviet occupation of Northern Bukovina](/wiki/Soviet_occupation_of_Bessarabia_and_Northern_Bukovina "Soviet occupation of Bessarabia and Northern Bukovina") in June 1940, he took refuge in [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest"), where he continued to work as a lawyer.

## Mayor of Cernăuți

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Traian_Popovici&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mayor of Cernăuți")]

In June 1941, Romania entered [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") on the side of the [Axis](/wiki/Axis_powers "Axis powers") and soon after re-established control over Bessarabia and Northern Bukovina. Military dictator [Ion Antonescu](/wiki/Ion_Antonescu "Ion Antonescu") requested him to become mayor of Cernăuți, but Popovici initially refused, unwilling to serve a [fascist](/wiki/Fascism "Fascism") government. He changed his mind, however, based on advice from his friends. A few days after acceptance, in early August 1941, he was ordered by [Alexandru Rioșanu](/w/index.php?title=Alexandru_Rio%C8%99anu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alexandru Rioșanu (page does not exist)") [[ro](https://ro.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexandru_Rio%C8%99anu "ro:Alexandru Rioșanu")], the Governor-General of [Bukovina](/wiki/Bukovina_Governorate "Bukovina Governorate"), to create a [ghetto](/wiki/Ghetto "Ghetto") for the Jews of Cernăuți, but Popovici refused to accept that part of the city's population could be confined behind [barbed wire](/wiki/Barbed_wire "Barbed wire") fences.

On August 30, 1941, Rioșanu died after an unsuccessful surgery, and he was succeeded as governor by General [Corneliu Calotescu](/wiki/Corneliu_Calotescu "Corneliu Calotescu"). On October 10, Calotescu announced his decision that all the Jews of Cernăuți must be [deported](/wiki/Deportation "Deportation") to [Transnistria](/wiki/Transnistria_(World_War_II) "Transnistria (World War II)"). After a long debate, Calotescu allowed Popovici to nominate 200 Jews who were to be exempted from deportation.[[6]](#cite_note-Lista-6) Unsatisfied with the modest concession, Popovici tried reaching Antonescu himself, this time arguing that Jews were of capital importance to Cernăuți's economy and requested a postponement until replacements could be found. As a result, he was allowed to expand the list, which covered 20,000 Jews in its final version.[[6]](#cite_note-Lista-6)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f4/TraianPopovici1.jpg/250px-TraianPopovici1.jpg)](/wiki/File:TraianPopovici1.jpg)

Grave of Popovici in [Colacu](/wiki/Fundu_Moldovei "Fundu Moldovei")

Popovici died in 1946 in the village of Colacu (part of [Fundu Moldovei](/wiki/Fundu_Moldovei "Fundu Moldovei") commune, in [Suceava County](/wiki/Suceava_County "Suceava County")), and was buried in the churchyard of the village, next to the [Colacu Wooden Church](/w/index.php?title=Colacu_Wooden_Church&action=edit&redlink=1 "Colacu Wooden Church (page does not exist)") [[ro](https://ro.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biserica_de_lemn_din_Colacu "ro:Biserica de lemn din Colacu")].

Due to Popovici's defense of Jews, his political adversaries nicknamed him "*jidovitul*" ("the turned-Jewish").

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Traian_Popovici&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Legacy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3e/Traian_Popovici_Righteous_Among_the_nations_tree_at_Yad_Vashem.jpg/250px-Traian_Popovici_Righteous_Among_the_nations_tree_at_Yad_Vashem.jpg)](/wiki/File:Traian_Popovici_Righteous_Among_the_nations_tree_at_Yad_Vashem.jpg)

Traian Popovici Righteous Among the Nations tree and plaque at [Yad Vashem](/wiki/Yad_Vashem "Yad Vashem")

Popovici is honored by [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel")'s [Yad Vashem](/wiki/Yad_Vashem "Yad Vashem") memorial as one of the *[Righteous Among the Nations](/wiki/Righteous_Among_the_Nations "Righteous Among the Nations")*, an honour given to non-Jews who behaved with heroism in trying to save Jews from the [genocide](/wiki/Genocide "Genocide") of the [Holocaust](/wiki/Holocaust "Holocaust").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Traian_Popovici&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of people who helped Jews during the Holocaust](/wiki/List_of_people_who_helped_Jews_during_the_Holocaust "List of people who helped Jews during the Holocaust")

## References
